old_value:
{"id":"lora_ipv6_mesh-mfir","title":"lichen-cli calls several CoAP paths the firmware doesn't expose (/presence /location /sos /rd)","description":"Surfaced while mapping the client stack for 7an. Beyond the now-fixed messaging bug (3b28ffd5: /messages->/msg/inbox, text->body), lichen-cli still calls CoAP resources with no firmware handler:
- 'presence' -> GET /presence (commands.rs) : firmware has no /presence; presence/neighbors live at /status/neighbors (and legacy /neighbors).
- 'position show' -> GET /location : firmware exposes /sensors/location (SenML) per spec 12-apps §18.2 and gateway /inbound/location; no bare /location.
- 'sos activate|cancel|status' -> GET/PUT/DELETE /sos : no firmware /sos resource exists at all.
- 'rd list|register|delete' -> /rd (CoAP RFC 9176 Resource Directory) : no firmware /rd resource.
- 'neighbors' -> GET /neighbors : works but is the legacy duplicate of /status/neighbors.

Each needs the same treatment as the messaging fix: verify the real firmware resource + payload (or confirm the feature is unimplemented on the node and gate/remove the subcommand), then route it through shared lichen-client types + paths. Position sharing in particular should get a shared lichen-client Position/SenML type (spec §18.2: lat/lon/alt/speed/heading, application/senml+cbor) consumed by both CLI and TUI (TUI has its own GeoPos in map.rs).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"mark.frost@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-07-19T00:50:44Z","created_by":"Mark Frost","updated_at":"2026-07-19T00:50:44Z"}

new_value: Updated lichen-cli to use correct CoAP paths via lichen-client::paths (STATUS_NEIGHBORS, SENSORS_LOCATION etc.) and shared types (Neighbors, Position); removed unimplemented subcommands (presence, sos, rd, position peers) that called non-existent firmware resources; updated command help, module docs, paths.rs comments.
